examples.title: Algorithm: Sensor Module Structure
examples.description: Designing a module for sensor interface in IoT systems
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateSensorModule
BEGIN
 MODULE SensorInterface
 FUNCTION init_sensor()
 Initialize hardware connection
 Return connection object
 
 FUNCTION read_sensor(sensor)
 Read raw data from sensor
 Convert to meaningful units
 Return processed value
 END MODULE
END
examples.explanation: Defines a module with initialization and data reading functions. init_sensor handles hardware setup, read_sensor performs data acquisition and conversion.

examples.title: Python: Sensor Interface Module
examples.description: Implementation of sensor interface module for temperature sensor
examples.explanation: Uses Adafruit library for DHT22 sensor. initialize_dht sets up hardware, read_temperature handles data collection with error retries.

examples.title: Algorithm: Modular IoT Application Flow
examples.description: Main program structure using multiple IoT modules
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MainIoTProgram
BEGIN
 IMPORT SensorModule
 IMPORT DataModule
 IMPORT CommsModule
 
 sensor = SensorModule.initialize()
 WHILE True
 raw_data = SensorModule.read(sensor)
 processed = DataModule.process(raw_data)
 CommsModule.transmit(processed)
 SLEEP 60
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Shows typical IoT workflow: initialize hardware, collect data, process, transmit, and repeat. Demonstrates separation of concerns through modules.

examples.title: Python: Main Weather Monitoring Program
examples.description: Integration of modules for complete IoT system
examples.code:
# File: main.py
from sensor import initialize_dht, read_temperature
from data_processor import celsius_to_fahrenheit
from mqtt_client import publish_data
import time

def main():
 dht_sensor = initialize_dht()
 
 while True:
 temp_c = read_temperature(dht_sensor)
 temp_f = celsius_to_fahrenheit(temp_c)
 publish_data('weather/temp', temp_f)
 time.sleep(60)

if __name__ == '__main__':
 main()
examples.explanation: Imports functionality from dedicated modules. Combines sensor reading, data conversion, and MQTT communication in a loop.

examples.title: Python: Using Standard Library Modules
examples.description: Leveraging Python's built-in modules for IoT tasks
examples.code:
# File: data_logger.py
import json
import time
from datetime import datetime

def create_log_entry(sensor_type, value):
 return {
 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
 'sensor': sensor_type,
 'value': value,
 'unit': '°C'
 }

def save_log(entry):
 with open('sensor_log.json', 'a') as f:
 f.write(json.dumps(entry) + '\n')
examples.explanation: Demonstrates using datetime for timestamps and json for serialization. Shows file handling for data logging.
